beta-objecttable-docs--docs.name: Docs
beta-objecttable-docs--docs.title: Beta/ObjectTable/Docs
beta-objecttable-docs--docs.content:
{/* ObjectTable.mdx — POC single-page docs structure. Pattern (loosely follows Mantine + base-ui): 1. Title + import 2. Description / when to use 3. Demo (linked to the Default story) 4. Usage (minimal example, prerequisites) 5. Examples (one or two Canvas embeds + link to Stories sidebar for the rest) 6. API Reference - Props → auto-generated from Api.ts via - CSS Variables → hand-authored, sourced from src/tokens/.css - Data attributes → hand-authored, sourced from .dataAttrs.ts - Selectors → guidance only (CSS modules are hashed; target data-attrs) 7. Accessibility Once doc generation tooling lands (§4.1 of the RFC), the CSS Variables and Data Attributes tables become generated blocks instead of hand-authored Markdown. */} import { Canvas, Controls, Meta, Source, Title } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; import * as ObjectTableStories from "./ObjectTable.stories"; # ObjectTable An OSDK-aware data table for ontology objects. Renders rows from an `ObjectType`, `ObjectSet`, or interface, and ships with column configuration, selection, sorting, pinning, resizing, inline editing, and an integrated filter surface. ## Demo ## Usage Pass an OSDK object type. ObjectTable will fetch rows, infer columns, and wire up sort / filter / selection out of the box. ; }`} /> ### Prerequisites - Wrap your app in `OsdkProvider` (see [Prerequisites](?path=/docs/prerequisites--docs)). - Import the package CSS and configure `@layer` order — see the [CSS Setup](?path=/docs/css-setup--docs) doc. ## Examples A few common variations. The full set is in the **Stories** sidebar. ### Selection ### Inline editing ## API Reference ### Props The full prop reference is auto-extracted from [`ObjectTableApi.ts`](https://github.com/palantir/osdk-ts/blob/main/packages/react-components/src/object-table/ObjectTableApi.ts). ### CSS Variables Override these on `OsdkThemeProvider` (or any ancestor) to customize the table. Token defaults come from `src/tokens/base-tokens/base.css`. export const cssVariableGroups = [ { title: "Outer border", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-border", "Outer border shorthand"], ["--osdk-table-border-color", "Outer border color"], ["--osdk-table-border-width", "Outer border width"], ], }, { title: "Cells", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-cell-color", "Cell text color"], ["--osdk-table-cell-divider", "Vertical divider between cells"], ["--osdk-table-cell-font", "Cell font shorthand"], ["--osdk-table-cell-padding", "Cell padding"], ], }, { title: "Editable cells", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-cell-editable-border", "Border on editable cells (read mode)"], ["--osdk-table-cell-edited-border", "Border on cells with pending edits"], ["--osdk-table-cell-edited-border-error", "Border on cells with edit errors"], ["--osdk-table-cell-input-bg", "Background of an editing input"], ["--osdk-table-edit-container-padding", "Padding of the inline edit container"], ["--osdk-table-edit-container-min-height", "Min height of the inline edit container"], ], }, { title: "Header row", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-header-bg", "Header row background"], ["--osdk-table-header-color", "Header text color"], ["--osdk-table-header-font", "Header font shorthand"], ["--osdk-table-header-height", "Header row height"], ["--osdk-table-header-divider", "Vertical divider between header cells"], ], }, { title: "Header menu button", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-header-menu-bg", "Background (rest)"], ["--osdk-table-header-menu-bg-hover", "Background (hover)"], ["--osdk-table-header-menu-bg-active", "Background (active)"], ["--osdk-table-header-menu-border", "Border"], ["--osdk-table-header-menu-color", "Icon/text color (rest)"], ["--osdk-table-header-menu-color-active", "Icon/text color (active)"], ["--osdk-table-header-menu-icon-color", "Icon color"], ["--osdk-table-header-menu-padding", "Padding"], ], }, { title: "Rows", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-row-bg-default", "Background (default state)"], ["--osdk-table-row-bg-alternate", "Background (zebra stripe)"], ["--osdk-table-row-bg-hover", "Background on hover"], ["--osdk-table-row-bg-active", "Background when selected/focused"], ["--osdk-table-row-border-color-hover", "Border color on hover"], ["--osdk-table-row-border-color-active", "Border color when active"], ["--osdk-table-row-divider", "Horizontal divider between rows"], ], }, { title: "Pinned columns and resizer", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-pinned-column-border", "Border separating pinned columns"], ["--osdk-table-resizer-color-hover", "Column resizer handle (hover)"], ["--osdk-table-resizer-color-active", "Column resizer handle (active)"], ], }, { title: "Loading state", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-skeleton-color-from", "Skeleton shimmer gradient start"], ["--osdk-table-skeleton-color-to", "Skeleton shimmer gradient end"], ], }, { title: "Column config dialog", vars: [ ["--osdk-table-column-config-dialog-min-width", "Min width"], ["--osdk-table-column-config-dialog-min-height", "Min height"], ["--osdk-table-column-config-visible-columns-bg", `Background of the "Visible columns" list`], ], }, ]; {cssVariableGroups.map(({ title, vars }) => (

{title}

{vars.map(([name, desc]) => ( ))}
Variable Controls
{name} {desc}
))} ### Data Attributes Stateful attributes you can target with CSS selectors. Prefer these over CSS class names — class names are CSS-module-hashed and not stable. export const dataAttrs = [ { name: "data-resizing", element: "", values: `"true" | "false"`, when: "Set while the user is dragging a column resizer", }, { name: "data-pinned", element: ", ", values: `"left" | "right" | "false"`, when: "Reflects each column's pin state", }, { name: "data-selected", element: "", values: `"true" | "false"`, when: "Set on selected rows", }, { name: "data-focused", element: "", values: `"true" | "false"`, when: "Set on the row currently focused by keyboard navigation", }, { name: "data-row-parity", element: "", values: `"even" | "odd"`, when: "Row striping; useful for zebra patterns at the consumer level", }, { name: "data-editable", element: "", values: `"true" | (absent)`, when: "Set when a cell can be edited in the current editMode", }, ]; {dataAttrs.map(({ name, element, values, when }) => ( ))}
Attribute Element Values When applied
{name} {element} {values} {when}
### Selectors ObjectTable uses CSS Modules internally, so class names are hashed at build time and **not** part of the public API. To style states or variants from a consuming app, target the documented data attributes instead. ## Accessibility - The table renders as a native `` with `` / `` / `` / `
` / `` for screen reader support. - Row focus is exposed via `data-focused` so keyboard navigation has a stable styling hook. - Editable cells use focusable form controls from `@base-ui/react`; tab order follows visual order. - When customizing colors via CSS variables, verify contrast against the active theme (light + dark).
